overview: Azorius Spirits is an aggro-based creature deck that blends proactive flying threats with control elements. It wins by applying early pressure with lords and cheap spirits while using utility creatures to counter spells, tap down blockers, and protect against board wipes. The deck relies on evasive damage and resource denial to close out games.
win_conditions: Dealing combat damage with high-power flying creatures
Squeezing the opponent out of resources through 2-for-1 utility spirits
Protecting the board with instant-speed indestructible to survive board wipes

keep_criteria: Hands with 1-2 lands and multiple cheap Spirit creatures
Hands containing lords like Empyrion Eagle or Supreme Phantom
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably duals or fetches), at least one 1-drop Spirit (e.g., Cryptbreaker or Spirited Phantom), and early interaction (No More Lies, spot removal, or bounce).
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only high-cost lords or no lands
Hands lacking early-game pressure or utility
Mulligan if you have 3+ lands with no early threats, multiple expensive cards with no mana base, or zero interaction against aggro/control.

examples.reason: Provides early lord pressure, cheap utility, and a land count suitable for an aggro curve

early_game: Play cheap Spirits to establish board presence
Cast lords like Empyrion Eagle or Supreme Phantom to boost power
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a 1-drop Spirit.
Turn 2: Develop board with another Spirit, use Cryptbreaker’s ability to bounce a blocker or disrupt a spell, or cast No More Lies to protect a threat or counter an opponent’s play.
Prioritize casting Spirits in response to opponent spells to maximize Rattlechains and Supreme Phantom triggers.
mid_game: Deploy utility Spirits like Rattlechains and Spell Queller to disrupt opponent's removal and spells
Use Shacklegeist and Nebelgast Herald to tap down opposing creatures
Keep 1-2 mana open for instant-speed interaction and Spirit triggers.
Don’t overcommit to the board if the opponent has sweepers.
Use the graveyard strategically with No More Lies to recycle threats or disrupt the opponent.
Commit threats when you have interaction to handle blockers or sweepers.
Hold back if the opponent has potential board wipes or can out-value you.
Use flying tokens to pressure planeswalkers or close games.
late_game: Flash in Selfless Spirit for instant-speed indestructible to survive board wipes and close the game
Supreme Phantom token generation, Cryptbreaker for targeted removal, and aerial assaults with flying Spirits.
Use No More Lies to cast a key Spirit from the graveyard to close the game or disrupt a combo.
Go all-in when you have board presence, interaction to handle sweepers, and the opponent is at low life or has no clear answer.
Commit when you can pressure through potential removal or when the opponent is tapped out.

key_cards.name: Empyrion Eagle
key_cards.notes: Provides power boost to Spirits for the aggro portion of the deck

key_cards.name: Supreme Phantom
key_cards.notes: Gives power in the air to flying Spirits

key_cards.name: Selfless Spirit
key_cards.notes: Makes the Spirit team indestructible to Wrath effects at instant speed

key_cards.name: Rattlechains
key_cards.notes: Stops removal spells and enables flash-in tricks for other Spirits

key_cards.name: Spell Queller
key_cards.notes: Acts as a counterspell to disrupt opponent's plays

key_cards.name: Shacklegeist
key_cards.notes: Taps down creatures without requiring mana investment or a Spirit to flash in

key_cards.name: Nebelgast Herald
key_cards.notes: Taps down creatures, often used in conjunction with flash-in Spirits

sequencing_tips: Know when to switch between aggro and control modes based on the board state
Flash in Spirits at instant speed to maximize combat tricks and utility
Prioritize tapping down opponent's key creatures before they can attack or block

Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board without interaction, mismanaging mana for instant-speed triggers, forgetting to use Cryptbreaker’s ability, and not tracking the opponent’s graveyard for No More Lies.
Unique interactions: Rattlechains can shut down key blockers mid-combat.
Supreme Phantom’s tokens are flying, making them hard to block.
No More Lies can be used offensively to cast a Spirit from the graveyard for a surprise trigger.

matchups.advice: Exploit their weakness by constantly tapping down their big creatures with Shacklegeist and Nebelgast Herald, forcing them to spend resources on Karametra's Blessing or lose tempo.

Use No More Lies to counter their counters or protect your spells. Cast threats in response to their counterspells to trigger Rattlechains/Supreme Phantom. Use Cryptbreaker to bounce their counterspell if possible. Don’t overcommit to one threat; trade up.
Apply early pressure to race them before they cast big spells. Use spot removal and counters to handle their acceleration. If they stabilize, use flying tokens and card advantage to out-value them.

Stabilize with early interaction, then transition to a control/aggro hybrid. Use No More Lies to protect your board or disrupt their combo. Once stabilized, use Supreme Phantom to generate tokens and close the game.
Pressure early, then use card advantage and recursion (No More Lies) to out-value them. Force them to use multiple answers. Use flying tokens to bypass board wipes.
Win the resource race. Prioritize keeping interaction, track their graveyard, and use No More Lies to steal their key Spirits. Win the trigger war by casting Spirits in response to their triggers.

common_threats: Oppressive combo decks (e.g., Inverter, Heliod Ballista)
Watch for sweepers, counterspells, and graveyard hate.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells.
Track if they’re playing around No More Lies by holding blue mana.
Watch for ramp/acceleration in midrange decks.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: an aggro-based creature deck, but with a twist... it is an aggro deck that can kill with big powered creatures in the air, but it also has a lot of control elements

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: kill with big powered creatures in the air
squeezing the opponent out of resources
Selfless Spirit even makes your team indestructible to Wrath effects

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: takes a pilot a lot of practice and experience to know when to switch from one mode to the other
making it that I can flash in spirits, for extra tricks, like flashing in a Selfless Spirit to give my team indestructible an instant speed

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: if I played something like Goblins, and the opponent plays a Supreme Verdict, it makes me feel so helpless
The only counter-play they have against it is Karametra's Blessing which provides hexproof
Rattlechains trying to stop a removal spell
